channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: TeamPCP hackers advertise Mistral AI code repos for sale
item.description: The TeamPCP hacker group is threatening to leak source code from the Mistral AI project unless a buyer is found for the data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers exploit auth bypass flaw in Burst Statistics WordPress plugin
item.description: Hackers are leveraging a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Burst Statistics to obtain admin-level access to websites. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SecurityScorecard Snags Driftnet to Level Up Threat Intelligence
item.description: The acquisition looks to boost visibility into third-party ecosystems, which are becoming a bigger concern as vectors for supply chain attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
item.description: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Cisco warns of new critical SD-WAN flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
item.description: Cisco is warning that a critical Catalyst SD-WAN Controller authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, was actively exploited in zero-day attacks that allowed attackers to gain administrat...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OpenAI confirms security breach in TanStack supply chain attack
item.description: OpenAI says two employees' devices were breached in the recent TanStack supply chain attack that impacted hundreds of npm and PyPI packages, causing the company to rotate code-signing certificates for...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows 11 and Microsoft Edge hacked at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
item.description: On the first day of Pwn2Own Berlin 2026, security researchers collected $523,000 in cash awards after exploiting 24 unique zero-days. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
item.description: Cisco has released updates to address a maximum-severity authentication bypass flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller that it said has been exploited in limited attacks. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stealer Backdoor Found in 3 Node-IPC Versions Targeting Developer Secrets
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about what has been described as "malicious activity" in newly published versions of node-ipc. According to Socket and StepSecurity, three different ve...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
item.description: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: PAN-OS RCE, Mythos cURL Bug, AI Tokenizer Attacks, and 10+ Stories
item.description: Everything is still on fire. This week feels dumb in the worst way — bad links, weak checks, fake help desks, shady forum posts, and people turning supply chain attacks into some cursed little game fo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 18-year-old NGINX vulnerability allows DoS, potential RCE
item.description: An 18-year-old flaw in the NGINX open-source web server, discovered using an autonomous scanning system, can be exploited for denial of service and, under certain conditions, remote code execution. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cyber-Enabled Cargo Crime: How Cybercrime Tradecraft is Used to Steal Freight
item.description: Cargo theft now starts with phishing emails and stolen credentials, not hijackings, to reroute and steal freight from supply chains. NMFTA outlines how cyber-enabled cargo crime is changing transporta...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukrainian Government With Geofenced PDF Phishing, Cobalt Strike
item.description: The Belarus-aligned threat group known as Ghostwriter has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting governmental organizations in Ukraine. Active since at least 2016, Ghostwriter has been li...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AI Drives Cybersecurity Investments, Widening 'Valley of Death'
item.description: In a role reversal, investment dollars in security startups exceeded the value of mergers and acquisitions in 1Q26 by more than $1 billion, a rare occurrence....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: KongTuke hackers now use Microsoft Teams for corporate breaches
item.description: Initial access broker KongTuke has moved to Microsoft Teams for social engineering attacks, taking as little as five minutes to gain persistent access to corporate networks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
item.description: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American facilities is one of 600 hits on manufacturers this year, as gangs increasingly target the sector for its low tolerance for downtime....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: PraisonAI CVE-2026-44338 Auth Bypass Targeted Within Hours of Disclosure
item.description: Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently disclosed security vulnerability in PraisonAI, an open-source multi-agent orchestration framework, within four hours of public disclos...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: How AI Hallucinations Are Creating Real Security Risks
item.description: AI hallucinations are introducing serious security risks into critical infrastructure decision-making by exploiting human trust through highly confident yet incorrect outputs. When an AI model lacks c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Dell confirms its SupportAssist software causes Windows BSOD crashes
item.description: Dell confirmed that its SupportAssist software is causing blue-screen crashes on some Windows systems following a wave of user reports about random reboots affecting Dell devices since Friday. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows Zero-Days Expose BitLocker Bypasses And CTFMON Privilege Escalation
item.description: An anonymous cybersecurity researcher who disclosed three Microsoft Defender vulnerabilities has returned with two more zero-days involving a BitLocker bypass and a privilege escalation impacting Wind...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US charges suspected Dream Market admin arrested in Germany
item.description: The alleged main administrator of Dream Market Incognito Market, one of the largest dark web marketplaces before its shutdown, has been indicted in the United States on money laundering charges. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Fragnesia Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges
item.description: Linux distros are rolling out patches for a new high-severity kernel privilege escalation vulnerability (known as Fragnasia and tracked as CVE-2026-46300) that allows attackers to run malicious code ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Fragnesia Linux Kernel LPE Grants Root Access via Page Cache Corruption
item.description: Details have emerged about a new variant of the recent Dirty Frag Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability that allows local attackers to gain root access, making it the third such bug to ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 18-Year-Old NGINX Rewrite Module Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open, including a critical flaw that remained undetected for 18 years. The vulnerability, disc...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: West Pharmaceutical says hackers stole data, encrypted systems
item.description: West Pharmaceutical Services disclosed that it was the target of a cyberattack that resulted in data exfiltration and system encryption. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Iranian hackers targeted major South Korean electronics maker
item.description: The Iran-linked hacking group MuddyWater (a.k.a. Seedworm, Static Kitten) launched a broad cyber-espionage campaign targeting at least nine high-profile organizations across multiple sectors and count...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Checkbox Assessments Aren't Fit to Measure Risk
item.description: Security governance needs to be more than an annual compliance exercise. New companies are emerging to address risk-management gaps in current audit tools....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
item.description: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Tables Turn on 'The Gentlemen' RaaS Gang With Data Leak
item.description: An OPSEC failure provides a window into what helped the ransomware group rise: a generous affiliate model, opportunistic TTPs, and an effective organizational structure....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
